title: Shark deterrent products lack safety standards | 7NEWS
description: Dozens of shark deterrent products are marketed in Australia, from magnetic bands to electric fields and bite-resistant wetsuits, but with no national safety standard it's difficult to determine which are effective. While some electric deterrents have been independently proven to work and Western Australia offers rebates for scientifically validated products, experts warn that small wearable magnets create limited magnetic fields and no technology can completely eliminate the risk of shark attacks, particularly from great white sharks. title: Europe deploys military forces to Middle East conflict | 7NEWS
description: European nations including the UK, France and Greece are deploying military assets to Cyprus following Iranian drone attacks on RAF Akrotiri. Britain is sending HMS Dragon destroyer and helicopters capable of intercepting drones, while Spain's refusal to participate has prompted Donald Trump to threaten trade cuts. Emirates flights from Dubai have resumed as Gulf countries restore limited air operations, offering hope for stranded travellers. title: Dramatic hot air balloon rescue in Texas | 7NEWS
description: Two passengers were rescued from more than 900 feet high after their hot air balloon collided with a mobile phone tower in Texas. Fourteen firefighters climbed a ladder barely a foot wide up the tower, taking more than an hour to safely bring the man and woman back to ground level. The dramatic rescue operation has been dubbed the 'Super Bowl of rope rescue'. title: Urgent call for funding to fix psychiatrist shortage | 7NEWS
description: Australia faces a critical mental health staffing crisis with only 16 psychiatrists per 100,000 people, particularly severe in regional and rural areas. The peak body representing psychiatrists is calling for $23 million in federal budget funding over two years to train young doctors and address workforce gaps created by an ageing psychiatrist population. The shortage threatens access to essential mental health services across the country. title: Kyle and Jackie O split after on-air stoush | 7NEWS
description: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O Henderson's breakfast radio partnership has ended, with ARN Media terminating their 10-year, $200 million contract with KIIS FM. Sandilands has been accused of serious misconduct and breach of contract following an on-air clash with Henderson last week, and has 14 days to rectify his actions. Henderson has been offered an alternative show with the company. description: The 2026 Sydney Royal Easter Show has unveiled its showbag lineup, featuring 418 options ranging from $5 to $38. title: Five-week leave push and retail robots coming | 7NEWS
description: Unions are pushing for Australian full-time workers to receive five weeks of annual leave, marking the first increase in more than five decades. Australian retail stores may replace paper price labels with digital tags that enable instant price updates and dynamic pricing. Harvey Norman boss Gerry Harvey plans to introduce robots to greet and service customers across his 200 retail stores within the next year or two.
